data.narrative:
Dream Security, a Series B defense and security company founded by Shalev Hulio, Sebastian Kurz, and Gil Dolev, carries a current post-money valuation of $1.5B following $100M+ in total raised capital. The valuation assessment draws from a comparable universe of 10 transactions—2 public peers (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private companies—all operating within the Defense & Security sector at equivalent or proximate stages. The peer set includes five named private benchmarks: Vannevar Labs ($1.5B), Harmattan AI ($1.4B), Code Metal ($1.2B), Castelion ($1.0B), and Defense Unicorns ($1.0B).

Two valuation methods proved applicable. Stage-progression analysis, applying a 1.9x multiplier to the $1.5B post-money base, yields $2.9B. Peer-median benchmarking across the seven same-stage, same-sector comparables produced a $1.0B median valuation, with a range spanning $300M to $1.5B. The disparity between these approaches reflects heterogeneity in company-specific risk profiles and market positioning within the peer cohort.

Triangulation across both methods supports a valuation range of $850M to $3.3B, with a median implied value of $1.9B. The current $1.5B valuation sits below the midpoint, suggesting either relative undervaluation against stage-typical multiples or divergence in risk-adjusted assessments. Key uncertainties include: differentiation relative to the five named private peers, capital efficiency relative to stage progression expectations, and sector-specific regulatory and procurement dependencies.
